sentences.en: On the last day before summer vacation, Mr. Pan played a game instead of a normal lesson: he called out a word, and we had to use it in a sentence about our own year.
sentences.zh: 暑假前的最後一天，潘老師沒上正常的課，改玩了一個遊戲：他念出一個字，我們就要用那個字造一句跟自己這一年有關的句子。

sentences.en: He started with "marry," and Bernice immediately shouted, "My aunt is going to marry a man who works in media!" which was, at least, completely true.
sentences.zh: 他先出了「marry」，Bernice 馬上大喊：「我姑姑要嫁給一個在媒體業工作的男人！」這句話至少是完全真實的。

sentences.en: For "wife," a boy said his uncle's future wife plans to move to the northern part of the country after the wedding.
sentences.zh: 輪到「wife」，有個男生說他叔叔未來的太太計劃婚後搬到國家的北部。

sentences.en: Mr. Pan gave us "forget," and I said I would never forget my very first diary entry, back on the first day of school.
sentences.zh: 潘老師出了「forget」，我說我永遠不會忘記自己第一篇日記，就是開學第一天寫的那篇。

sentences.en: For "tire," William's teacher apparently said a similar game happened at his school, and he answered that giant robots never get tired, only their batteries do.
sentences.zh: 輪到「tire」，聽說 William 學校也玩了類似的遊戲，他回答巨大機器人永遠不會累，只有電池會沒電。

sentences.en: Someone got "dirty" and simply pointed at William's shoes from the farm field trip months ago, and the whole class laughed.
sentences.zh: 有人拿到「dirty」，直接指了指幾個月前農場校外教學時 William 那雙鞋，全班都笑了。

sentences.en: For "kill," a boy said this year's math homework nearly killed his motivation completely, which felt relatable to most of us.
sentences.zh: 輪到「kill」，有個男生說今年的數學作業差點徹底殺死他的動力，這句話讓我們大部分人都心有戚戚焉。

sentences.en: I got "eraser," and I mentioned I went through at least ten of them this year, mostly because of math mistakes.
sentences.zh: 我拿到「eraser」，我說我今年至少用掉了十個橡皮擦，大部分都是因為數學錯誤。

sentences.en: For "file," Bernice said she finally organized her school papers into one single file instead of one giant messy pile.
sentences.zh: 輪到「file」，Bernice 說她終於把學校的紙本整理成一個檔案夾，而不是一大堆亂七八糟的紙。

sentences.en: Someone used "legal" by saying it becomes legal to get a part-time job once you turn a certain age, which sparked a small debate about fairness.
sentences.zh: 有人用「legal」造句，說到了一定年紀打工才合法，這引發了一場關於公不公平的小小辯論。

sentences.en: For "medical," I mentioned our school health check-up, and how my medical card said "healthy, active, good posture."
sentences.zh: 輪到「medical」，我提到我們學校的健康檢查，還有我的醫療卡上寫著「健康、活潑、姿勢良好」。

sentences.en: Mr. Pan gave us "poor" and "rich," and one girl said a diary makes you feel rich in memories, even on days you feel poor in everything else.
sentences.zh: 潘老師出了「poor」跟「rich」，有個女生說日記能讓你在回憶上感覺很富有，就算其他方面感覺很貧乏的日子也一樣。

sentences.en: For "self," I said keeping this diary all year taught me more about my own self than any single test ever did.
sentences.zh: 輪到「self」，我說寫這整年的日記，讓我對自己的認識比任何一次考試都還要多。

sentences.en: Someone got "shake" and described shaking hands with the museum guide during our old town field trip, which felt very grown-up at the time.
sentences.zh: 有人拿到「shake」，描述了老城區校外教學時跟博物館導覽員握手的事，當時感覺自己超成熟。

sentences.en: For "shower," William apparently told his teacher that after every soccer practice, a cold shower feels like the best part of the whole day.
sentences.zh: 輪到「shower」，聽說 William 跟他老師說，每次踢完足球，一場冷水澡感覺是一整天最棒的部分。

sentences.en: Mr. Pan used "universe" himself, saying that to him, a classroom full of curious kids sometimes feels like its own tiny universe.
sentences.zh: 潘老師自己用了「universe」造句，說對他來說，一間坐滿好奇小孩的教室，有時候感覺就像它自己的一個小宇宙。

sentences.en: For "weak," Bernice admitted her only weak subject all year was math, and I told her, honestly, mine was exactly the same.
sentences.zh: 輪到「weak」，Bernice 坦承她今年唯一的弱科是數學，我老實跟她說我的弱科也一模一樣。

sentences.en: Someone used "available" by saying our teacher was always available to answer questions, even silly ones, which we all quietly appreciated.
sentences.zh: 有人用「available」造句，說我們老師隨時都願意回答問題，就算是很蠢的問題也一樣，我們私下都很感激。

sentences.en: For the leftover words, Mr. Pan simply read a quick, funny list out loud: a snowy northern town, a slim menu with no available seats, a fan blowing on a hot afternoon, a shall we begin here, and a school with no teens allowed to skip class, and told us that was quite enough vocabulary for one final day.
sentences.zh: 剩下的字，潘老師乾脆快速念了一份好笑的清單：一座下雪的北方小鎮、一份沒有空位的簡單菜單、一台在炎熱下午吹著的電扇，一句「我們開始吧」，還有一所不准青少年翹課的學校，並說今天最後一天這些字彙已經夠多了。

sentences.en: We ended the game laughing, and Mr. Pan said this diary project, more than any test this year, showed him who we really are.
sentences.zh: 我們在笑聲中結束了這個遊戲，潘老師說這個日記計畫，比今年任何一次考試都更讓他看清楚我們真正是什麼樣的人。

quiz.question: What does Coco say for the word "forget"?
options: She will never forget her first diary entry.
She forgot how to spell the word.

quiz.question: What do both Coco and Bernice admit is their weak subject?
What does Mr. Pan say the diary project showed him?
options: That everyone should quit writing diaries.
That the students need more tests.
